name: Q712435
abstract: Arthur Bernstein (February 4, 1909 – January 4, 1964) was an American jazz double bassist. Born in Brooklyn, New York, he started his musical career playing cello on board cruise ships to South America, and also studied law at New York University.

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: your band, Chauncey Morehouse and his Swing Six... Artie Bernstein on string bass and you on the drums

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: my two favorite bass players were Pops Foster... and Artie Bernstein, who I happened to hear along about that time... I was very impressed with that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Does Artie Bernstein's name ring a bell to you? ... [INTERVIEWER] Bass player with Redman.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmie Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Oh, it's Artie Bernstein on bass. Sid or Artie? [INTERVIEWEE] Sid, I think.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Chace
about_quotes.evidence: you can name a string of them john they were you know Artie Bernstein

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: her brother was the bass player, Artie Bernstein, with Benny's band... I knew the woman he was married to, Bonnie Lake I knew very well. She was a good friend of my brother-in-law's, Artie's.
